article.title: Federal court affirms patient right of access fee cap; sets 25-day designated record set deadline
article.summary: A federal court has upheld OCR's interpretation of the patient right of access provision (45 CFR 164.524). The ruling affirms the $6.50 flat-fee cap for electronic copies and confirms covered entities have at most 25 days (with a one-time 30-day extension) to respond to a record set request.
article.rawContent: The Eleventh Circuit Court of Appeals affirmed OCR's HIPAA Right of Access Initiative, ruling that covered entities cannot impose per-page or 'review' fees that effectively chill the patient's access right. The court emphasized that the 30-day response window in 45 CFR 164.524(b)(2) is not a presumption but a ceiling, and that practices should respond promptly when records are readily available. Practices that have not audited their record-release procedures or fee schedules in the past 12 months should expect renewed OCR enforcement focus on this provision.
